objective: Review the HELLO-BUG-002 Path B fix: hello.py:15 guard restored to `if shout:` plus a standalone CliRunner regression script, against the actual code and the approved plan.
key_changes: hello.py:15 guard confirmed as `if shout:` so --shout upper-cases both formal and informal greetings; `greeting.upper()` is a real call.
Net git diff of hello.py is empty by design (HEAD already had the fix); the regression test is the substantive artifact.
New standalone tests/test_shout_formal.py (no pytest) driving CliRunner; exits non-zero on failure, robust hello.py import via repo root on sys.path.
findings: Read hello.py directly: line 15 is `if shout:`; informal path `Hello, NAME!` intact; `for _ in range(count)` loop unchanged and outside the shout guard (no FORGE-BUG-007 regression).
Ran `python3 tests/test_shout_formal.py`: all 4 cases PASS, exit 0.
Discrimination proven: against a reintroduced buggy guard, the two formal-shout cases FAIL (Greetings, Alice != GREETINGS, ALICE), exit 1; controls still pass. Genuine guard, not a tautology.
Expected strings correct: formal+shout=GREETINGS, ALICE (no trailing !), informal shout=HELLO, ALICE!, formal=Greetings, Alice, formal+shout+count2 repeats the shouted formal line twice.
Test has no pytest dependency and resolves hello.py robustly (repo root on sys.path) regardless of cwd; matches null config.commands.test.
git diff hello.py empty confirmed — expected per plan; bug.status (in-progress) and bug.path untouched by the change.
No security surface: name flows through f-strings and .upper() only, no shell/eval. Type hints present, no unused imports, single-file click architecture preserved.
